decks.notes: The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. Using cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Subtlety to disrupt opponents' strategies while generating card advantage with cards like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by efficiently dealing with threats and drawing additional cards. Protection and removal are key components of this control deck to outlast opponents and secure victory through attrition. The Dimir Control archetype specializes in controlling the game and maintaining card advantage to outmaneuver opponents.

The main concept of this Modern 1 vs 1 Ruby Storm combo deck is to generate mana quickly with cards like Pyretic Ritual, Desperate Ritual, and Ruby Medallion to cast powerful spells like Past in Flames and Grapeshot. This deck aims to storm off by chaining together spells with Ral, Monsoon Mage and utilizing cards like Wish and Glimpse the Impossible to draw and play more cards in the same turn. The deck also includes some land fetch cards like Scalding Tarn and Arid Mesa to ensure consistency in mana production.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler. It also has the potential to lock opponents out of the game with cards like No More Lies and Orim's Chant, while using cards like Wrenn and Six for value and card advantage. The deck aims to control the board and the opponent's actions, while setting up win conditions like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury or Wrath of the Skies to close out the game.

The main strategy of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to control the board through targeted removal like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict, counter key spells with cards like Counterspell and Spell Snare, and generate card advantage with cards like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck also utilizes land destruction with Field of Ruin and Undercity Sewers to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The goal is to outvalue the opponent and win through attrition.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to aggressively play creatures and planeswalkers to attack the opponent quickly and efficiently. With a focus on small, fast creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by powerful cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm adds additional versatility and removal options, while cards like Blood Moon and Voice of Victory provide disruption and protection for the aggressive strategy.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Guide of Souls and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, supported by powerful spells like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm. The deck aims to quickly establish a strong board presence and apply pressure early in the game, while also having the flexibility to control the board with cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory. By combining efficient creatures and impactful spells, this deck looks to close out games swiftly and efficiently.

This deck focuses on assembling powerful combos using creatures as tools. By utilizing cards like Green Sun's Zenith, Devoted Druid, and Vizier of Remedies, players can generate infinite mana and pump out massive creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth for a game-winning turn. Leyline of Abundance helps ramp up mana production, while cards like Duskwatch Recruiter and Agatha's Soul Cauldron provide additional value and card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of efficient creatures, strong synergy, and explosive combos to achieve victory in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck revolves around ramping up mana quickly using cards like Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. It also includes control elements such as Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with giant creatures while maintaining control of the board through removal spells and disruptive abilities, ultimately leading to victory through sheer power and efficiency.

The key strategy of this deck is to quickly assemble combo pieces, such as Basking Broodscale and Blade of the Bloodchief, to create a powerful board state. Using cards like Devourer of Destiny and Malevolent Rumble to control the board while ramping into big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. The deck utilizes cards like Kozilek's Command and Ugin's Labyrinth for disruption and removal, with support from cards like Formidable Speaker for additional value. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of combo pieces and powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on controlling the game and disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of powerful Eldrazi creatures, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. The deck aims to assemble UrzaTron (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate massive amounts of mana to cast game-winning threats like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. The inclusion of cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Trinisphere adds disruption to the opponent's game plan, while Mind Stone and Expedition Map help with mana ramp and consistency. The deck aims to control the board and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing creatures with low mana costs to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy is to use cards that allow for efficient damage output, such as Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment, while also providing support and card advantage through cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer. The deck also includes removal and protection spells like Thraben Charm and Ranger-Captain of Eos to maintain board control. Overall, the Boros Aggro deck aims to apply constant pressure and finish the game swiftly.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the Amulet of Vigor and Primeval Titan combo to generate a large amount of mana and land drops. The deck utilizes cards like Arboreal Grazer, Lotus Field, and Simic Growth Chamber to ramp up and search for key pieces like Urza's Saga and Scapeshift. Boseiju, Who Endures helps protect key combo pieces from countermagic, while cards like Malevolent Rumble and Cultivator Colossus provide additional support and disruption. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful land drops and threats, ultimately leading to a quick victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate a high amount of mana and cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also includes control elements like Thought-Knot Seer and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With access to cards like Expedition Map and Mind Stone for ramp, this deck aims to overpower the opponent with large threats while controlling the board with removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command.

This deck focuses on using instant speed reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to cheat powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ulamog, the Defiler into play. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog help to dig for combo pieces and disruption. The deck also runs cards like Solitude for removal and Faithful Mending for card draw and graveyard recursion. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to reanimate a big threat and overwhelm the opponent before they can react.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It utilizes a mix of different colors to maximize its efficiency and versatility. The key strategy is to establish a strong board presence early on, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal spells and countermagic. The deck aims to close out the game quickly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. By combining powerful creatures with efficient removal and disruption, it looks to outpace and outmaneuver its opponents to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By efficiently utilizing mana fixing and powerful multicolor creatures, the deck is able to deal significant damage early on in the game. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding help to support the aggro strategy by boosting the power of the creatures and providing removal options for opposing threats. The inclusion of Tribal Flames and Tribal Flames allows for even more versatility in dealing damage and keeping up the pressure on the opponent.
